description: Adela v1 — Client-facing escalation & Q&A agent for FDE teams. Standalone web app where enterprise customers get documentation-grounded Q&A and structured escalation with rich context capture. Vendor FDE teams get a real-time escalation dashboard, resolution library, and per-account health view. Slack integration for automatic issue detection. Built with Next.js/React (TS) frontend on Vercel, Node.js (TS) backend, Python agent services on AWS ECS, Convex for data/realtime/vector search, OpenAI GPT-4.1 for classification and Q&A, Clerk for auth. Multi-tenant: vendor = tenant, customer account = sub-tenant. Day-one value scope only — no autonomous resolution in v1.

techStack.agents: Python 3.11+ for AI agent orchestration (classifier, Q&A, context builder, doc ingestion)
techStack.database: Convex (structured data, real-time subscriptions, vector search)
techStack.ai: OpenAI GPT-4.1 for classification/Q&A/reasoning, text-embedding-3-small (1536d) for embeddings
techStack.auth: Clerk (orgs map to vendor tenants, invite-link onboarding for customers)
techStack.infrastructure: AWS ECS/Lambda for Python services, Vercel for frontend, GitHub Actions CI/CD

designSystem.aesthetic: Dark editorial sophistication — serif headlines, monospace labels, sage green accents on deep black. Calm, information-dense, trustworthy.

multiTenancy.model: Vendor as tenant (Clerk Org), customer accounts as sub-tenants within vendor. Resolution procedures scoped to vendor level for cross-account pattern recognition.
multiTenancy.isolation: Every Convex query filters by vendorId (vendor users) or accountId (customer users). No cross-vendor or cross-account data access.

userStories.title: Vendor sign-up and org creation
userStories.description: As a vendor admin, I want to create a Adela account so I can start onboarding my team and customers.
acceptanceCriteria: Clerk-powered sign-up flow (email + password)
Org name and company URL collected
Vendor record created in Convex with clerkOrgId, name, slug
Redirects to onboarding wizard on completion
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Clerk Organizations map 1:1 to vendor tenants.

userStories.title: Slack workspace integration
userStories.description: As a vendor admin, I want to connect my Slack workspace and select channels to monitor so Adela can detect customer issues automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: OAuth flow connects Slack workspace, stores bot token encrypted in Convex
Admin sees list of all workspace channels and can select/deselect which to monitor
Selected channels associated with correct customer account
Slack bot added to selected channels and begins receiving events
Webhook signature verification using SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET on every request
Minimal OAuth scopes requested (channels:history, channels:read, chat:write)
Integration status shown on dashboard (connected/disconnected)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Slack webhook handler should normalize messages into a generic IncomingSignal format to future-proof for additional channels (email, Jira, etc).

userStories.title: Documentation ingestion and indexing
userStories.description: As a vendor admin, I want to upload product documentation so the Q&A agent can answer customer questions from day one.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can upload markdown and PDF files
Files chunked into passages, embedded with OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (1536d)
Chunks stored in Convex document_chunks table with vector index filtered by vendorId
Progress indicator shows processing status
Admin sees count of indexed documents/chunks after processing
Uploading additional docs appends to existing index, no overwrites
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This seeds the Q&A agent — quality of chunking directly affects answer quality. v1 requires manual re-upload when docs change.

userStories.title: Customer account creation and invite flow
userStories.description: As a vendor admin, I want to create customer accounts and generate invite links so enterprise users can onboard themselves.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin creates account with: company name, primary contact email, associated Slack channels
Unique invite code generated per account
Invite link can be copied and shared with customer
Customer signs up via invite link → Clerk auth → auto-associated with correct vendor + account
Customer user gets role 'customer_user' with accountId set
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Customer Q&A — ask a question
userStories.description: As an enterprise customer user, I want to ask questions about my vendor's product and get immediate documentation-grounded answers.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat interface with conversation history (persisted per user via Convex)
Text input with 'Ask anything about [Vendor Product]...' placeholder
On submit: typing indicator shown, question sent to Python Q&A service
Semantic search against vendor doc vector index, retrieves top-k chunks
GPT-4.1 generates grounded answer with source citations (doc title + section)
If confidence below threshold (default 0.6): auto-escalates and informs customer
Response time < 5 seconds for documentation-grounded answers
Fallback: if Python service unavailable, message queued, customer told team will follow up
Test with 10 sample questions — accuracy > 80% for questions with clear doc answers
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Q&A agent system prompt must only answer from provided documentation, refuse general questions, cite sources. Rate limit: 20 questions/hour per customer user. Sanitize inputs for prompt injection.

userStories.title: Slack issue detection and classification
userStories.description: As a vendor FDE, I want Adela to automatically detect issues from customer Slack channels so I don't have to manually monitor every channel.
acceptanceCriteria: Messages in monitored channels received via Slack webhook → Node.js handler
Handler normalizes to IncomingSignal format, forwards to Python classifier
GPT-4.1 classifies: is this an issue? If yes → title, description, severity, category
Non-issue messages ignored (no Issue record created)
Issue record created in Convex with classification and Slack context attached
Classification completes within 3 seconds
Test with 20 sample messages (10 issues, 10 non-issues) — precision > 85%, recall > 75%
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: For high-volume channels (100+ messages/day), may need secondary filter or batching — validate with design partners.

userStories.title: Structured escalation with context package
userStories.description: As a vendor FDE, I want escalations to arrive with a full context package so I can start investigating immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: On escalation trigger: Python context builder assembles summary, classification, reproduction context, related Slack messages, related prior issues, suggested investigation path
GPT-4.1 generates structured markdown summary
Escalation record created in Convex with full contextPackage object
Real-time notification to vendor dashboard via Convex subscription
Optional notification to vendor Slack channel
Escalation creation completes within 5 seconds of trigger
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Primary day-one value prop. Invest disproportionate effort in context builder prompt engineering. If context packages are low-quality, the product fails.

userStories.title: Vendor dashboard — real-time escalation feed
userStories.description: As a vendor FDE lead, I want a real-time feed of all open escalations across accounts so I can triage and assign work.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows all open escalations sorted by recency (default)
Each card: account name, issue title, semantic classification, severity badge, context preview, time-since indicator
Filters: by account, severity, status, date range
Sort options: recency, severity
Cards update in real-time via Convex subscription — no page refresh
'View Full Context' expands inline or opens detail view
Empty state: 'No open escalations — your agent is handling it.'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Escalation resolution and procedure recording
userStories.description: As a vendor FDE, I want to mark an escalation as resolved and record the resolution steps so the team can reference them later.
acceptanceCriteria: 'Mark Resolved' button prompts for resolution notes (free text, markdown supported)
On submit: escalation status → resolved, linked issue status → resolved
resolution_procedure record auto-created with: title (auto-suggested from classification), steps, source escalation/issue, affected account
Procedure embedding generated and stored for semantic matching in v2
Resolution appears in Resolution Library
Customer sees updated issue status in their portal
Thumbs up/down quality rating on escalation for training signal
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Open question: free text only vs optional structured fields (root cause, fix steps, affected component). Free text faster to ship but harder to match in v2.

userStories.title: Resolution library — read-only view
userStories.description: As a vendor FDE, I want to browse all recorded resolution procedures so I can check if a similar issue has been solved before.
acceptanceCriteria: Library page lists all resolution procedures for the vendor
Each entry: title, category, steps (truncated), accounts affected count, date created
Full-text search across titles and steps
Click entry → full resolution details
Empty state: 'Resolutions will appear here as your team resolves escalated issues.'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Read-only in v1. No editing or approval workflows. Sets the stage for autonomous resolution in v2.

userStories.title: Account health view
userStories.description: As a vendor FDE lead, I want to see per-account health metrics so I can identify which customers need the most attention.
acceptanceCriteria: Accounts page lists all customer accounts: name, total issues, open escalations, avg resolution time, last activity
Click through → account detail with issue history, escalation history, Slack activity summary
Sortable by open escalations count and last activity
Empty state: 'Add your first customer account to get started.'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Customer issue list and detail view
userStories.description: As an enterprise customer user, I want to see all my reported issues and their current status.
acceptanceCriteria: Issues page shows all issues for customer's account
Each card: title, severity badge, status, timestamp, short description
Click through → issue detail with activity timeline
Resolved issues show resolution notes
Empty state: 'No issues yet — that's a good sign.'
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

scope: Day-one value only — no autonomous L1 resolution in v1
Resolution recording, not replay — library is read-only reference for humans
No vendor product instrumentation — issue detection from Slack and customer Q&A only
Slack only — no GitHub, Linear, Jira, or CRM integration

resources.infrastructure: Vercel Pro, Convex Pro, AWS minimal (1–2 ECS tasks), Clerk Starter/Pro

aiLlm: Graceful degradation if AI service unavailable
Cache embeddings for identical documents and common Q&A pairs
Respect OpenAI tier-based rate limits; implement queuing if needed

security.auth: Clerk handles all auth. Clerk Organizations map 1:1 to vendor tenants. Customer invite-link onboarding scoped to specific account.
security.secrets: OPENAI_API_KEY, SLACK_BOT_TOKEN (per-vendor encrypted), SLACK_SIGNING_SECRET, CLERK_SECRET_KEY, CONVEX_DEPLOY_KEY — all server-side only, never in git.
security.rowLevelSecurity: Every query filters by vendorId or accountId. No cross-vendor or cross-account query paths.
aiSecurity: System prompt restricts Q&A agent to only answer from provided documentation
Output validation for hallucination — low grounding scores not surfaced
Daily spend monitoring with alert at 2x average, hard kill switch at monthly cap
Zero data retention requested from OpenAI

successMetrics.designPartners: 2–3 vendors onboarded within 4 weeks of launch
successMetrics.qaDeflectionRate: > 40% of customer questions resolved without escalation in month 1
successMetrics.timeToInvestigate: < 15 min context gathering per escalation (vs 30–60 min baseline)
successMetrics.issueDetectionPrecision: > 85% true positive rate on Slack-detected issues
successMetrics.resolutionLibraryGrowth: > 20 procedures per vendor in first 2 months
successMetrics.uptime: 99.5% for customer-facing Q&A and vendor dashboard

openQuestions: Confidence threshold calibration — default 0.6, needs tuning with design partners
Slack channel volume — high-volume channels may need secondary filter or batching
Resolution procedure structure — free text vs optional structured fields for v2 matching
Customer-facing agent identity — Adela branding vs vendor branding
Vendor doc freshness — v1 manual re-upload, consider webhook for hosted docs in v1.1
Escalation notification routing — per-FDE routing by account/category likely v1.1
Data retention policy — must define before fintech design partners
